headline: Alphabet Stock Joins the Dow but It’s Rising for a Different Reason
summary: The shares were up 1.2% in premarket trading.  The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.

headline: Nvidia Stock Rises Amid Signs of Strong AI Chip Demand
summary: Nvidia  stock was rising early Monday after a difficult week during which the chip maker’s shares fell below its recent floor of $200.  The shares were up 1.1% at $194.33 in premarket trading.  Australian firm Firmus signed an eight-year partnership with Nvidia to build a dedicated 360 megawatt AI facility in Batam, an Indonesia island off the coast of Singapore, it said in a statement Monday.

Australian firm Firmus signed an eight-year partnership with Nvidia to build a dedicated 360 megawatt AI facility in Batam, an Indonesia island off the coast of Singapore, it said in a statement Monday.
